fields.name: pgpid
fields.description: unique identifier for the document
fields.title: PGPID

fields.name: url
fields.description: public view of this document on PGP  website
fields.title: URL

fields.name: iiif_urls
fields.description: link to the document/fragment images if hosted in IIIF format
fields.title: IIIF URLs

fields.name: fragment_urls
fields.description: link to the document/fragment images if hosted in format that is not IIIF
fields.title: Fragment URLs

fields.name: shelfmark
fields.description: the library’s name for the document and the name cited in scholarship
fields.title: Shelfmark

fields.name: multifragment
fields.description: yes/no for whether this shelfmark contains multiple small fragments of text

fields.name: side
fields.description: recto or verso, indicates which side of the fragment this document is composed on
fields.title: Side

fields.name: region
fields.description: when multiple documents are composed on the same fragment, this field can be used to indicate which part of the fragment is associated with this PGPID. Many scholars use lower case letters (a, b, c) to indicate the correct region in reading order
fields.title: Region

fields.name: type
fields.description: the PGP divides its material into 9 document types (credit/financial instrument, inscription, legal, legal query or responsa, letter, list or table, literary, paraliterary, and state). These types correspond to the form and content of the document
fields.title: Document Type

fields.name: tags
fields.description: tags added by research assistants interested in particular topics (such as medicine)
fields.title: Tags

fields.name: description
fields.description: an English-language description of the document
fields.title: Description

fields.name: scholarship_records
fields.description: list of associated secondary sources and editions of the document
fields.title: Scholarship Records

fields.name: shelfmarks_historic
fields.title: Historic Shelfmarks
fields.description: old and alternate ways of referring to this document (to aid readers of older scholarly texts find the current shelfmarks)

fields.name: languages_primary
fields.description: primary language(s) necessary to read the document
fields.title: Primary Languages

fields.name: languages_secondary
fields.description: noting additional languages the document occasionally uses
fields.title: Secondary Languages

fields.name: language_note
fields.description: free text field to note strange use of diacritics, spellings, etc.
fields.title: Language Notes

fields.name: doc_date_original
fields.description: the month and day listed on the document in its original form. Months are often from the Hebrew calendar and sometimes the Muslim calendar
fields.title: Date on Document

fields.name: doc_date_calendar
fields.description: calendars from this period include the Jewish calendar (Anno Mundi), Seleucid calendar, Muslim calendar (Hijri), and the tax calendar (kharaji)
fields.title: Calendar of Date on Document

fields.name: doc_date_standard
fields.description: the date listed on the document converted to its equivalent in the Common Era. 	A slash is used to represent a range of dates.
fields.title: Standardized Document Date

fields.name: inferred_date_display
fields.description: computer legible version of scholar’s inferred dating of the document
fields.title: Inferred Dating of Document

fields.name: inferred_date_standard
fields.title: Inferred Dating of document, Standardized
fields.description: computer legible version of scholar’s inferred dating of the document

fields.name: inferred_date_rationale
fields.title: Inferred Date Rationale
fields.description: choice of options for why this dating is assumed: the coinage mentioned, person mentioned, paleographic features, event mentioned, or other.

fields.name: inferred_date_notes
fields.title: Inferred Date Notes
fields.description: additional explanation of why we should assume the document was written on this date/in this date range

fields.name: initial_entry
fields.description: when this document was first added to the PGP
fields.title: Initial Entry into PGP

fields.name: last_modified
fields.description: most recent edit of the document in the database
fields.title: Entry Last Modified

fields.name: input_by
fields.description: all users who have edited this database entry
fields.title: Input By

fields.name: library
fields.description: holding library of the fragment(s) this document is on
fields.title: Library

fields.name: collection
fields.description: collection within the library where fragment(s) are held
fields.title: Library Collection

fields.name: has_transcription
fields.description: yes/no is there a digital edition on the PGP
fields.title: Has Transcription

fields.name: has_translation
fields.description: yes/no is there a digital translation on the PGP
fields.title: Has Translation

fields.name: document
fields.description: the name of the document or person page the footnote is featured on

fields.name: document_id
fields.description: the PGPID (for documents) or person identifier number (for database use only)

fields.name: source
fields.description: the published or unpublished author and source of the footnoted content

fields.name: source_slug
fields.description: Re-expressing the name with continuous letters that may be used as part of a URL

fields.name: location
fields.description: where in a published source this footnote derives from.

fields.name: doc_relation
fields.description: how is the footnote related to the document (discussion/translation/transcription)

fields.name: emendations
fields.description: Revisions or corrections

fields.name: notes
fields.description: an open-text field, sometimes features additional information about the source or footnote. Often used to annotate digital editions, for example “with minor emendations by Alan Elbaum” means that Alan reviewed the digital edition and fixed a few spelling errors or populated words that would not have been visible to the original scholar working off of a photocopied image.

fields.name: url
fields.description: link to the footnote’s source, if relevant. Mostly used for Goitein’s unpublished index cards and their discussions of documents

fields.name: content
fields.description: the content of digital  transcriptions and translations for the document.

fields.name: shelfmark
fields.description: the library’s name for the document and the name cited in scholarship

fields.name: pgpids
fields.description: the unique identifier(s) for the documents on this fragment

fields.name: shelfmarks_historic
fields.description: old and alternate ways of referring to this document (to aid readers of older scholarly texts find the current shelfmarks).

fields.name: collection
fields.description: abbreviated version of collection and library information as seen in the shelfmark

fields.name: library
fields.description: holding library of the fragment

fields.name: library_abbrev
fields.description: abbreviation for the library name seen in shelfmarks and on the PGP

fields.name: collection_name
fields.description: collection within the library where fragment is held

fields.name: collection_abbrev
fields.description: abbreviation for the library name seen in shelfmarks and on the PGP

fields.name: url
fields.description: holding library’s page for this fragment, if available

fields.name: iiif_url
fields.description: link to the fragment image IIIF manifest, if available

fields.name: is_multifragment
fields.description: yes/no if there are multiple fragments under the same shelfmark

fields.name: created
fields.description: date added to PGP
date of addition to current database

fields.name: last_modified
fields.description: date last modified in PGP
date of last modification

fields.name: provenance_display
fields.description: source of the fragment (e.g. Geniza, non-Geniza ..etc)

fields.name: provenance
fields.description: earliest known / publication history

fields.name: material_support
fields.description: the physical material from which the fragment is believed to be manufactured / made

fields.name: name
fields.description: the main display name for the person

fields.name: name_variants
fields.description: additional names and spellings in different languages

fields.name: gender
fields.description: male, female, or unknown

fields.name: social_roles
fields.description: Enslaved person, Jewish communal official, Jewish community member, Muslim judicial official, Other, State official

fields.name: auto_date_range
fields.description: date range of person’s life populated from dated documents the person is connected to

fields.name: manual_date_range
fields.description: scholar input date range of person’s life from non-Geniza sources

fields.name: description
fields.description: brief description of the person’s life or role in the documents

fields.name: tags
fields.description: tags added by research assistants interested in particular topics (such as being an individual related to the Indian Ocean trade [IB])

fields.name: related_people_count
fields.description: number of individuals this person is connected to

fields.name: family_traces_roots_to
fields.description: the place the person comes from, often known through their kunya (eg. Abū Naṣr al-Baṣrī	may not have been born in Basra, Iraq, but his name indicates his family was from there)

fields.name: home_base
fields.description: location where person lived

fields.name: traveled_to
fields.description: known locations of travel for trade or pilgrimage

fields.name: related_documents_count
fields.description: number of documents associated with this person

fields.name: url
fields.description: link to this person’s page on the PGP public site
